claim: Linear is a product development system for teams and agents.
Linear is purpose‑built for planning and building products and is designed for the AI era.
Linear incorporates AI agents that can draft PRDs, push PRs, and perform other workflow tasks alongside humans.
Linear aims to reduce noise and restore momentum to help teams ship with high velocity and focus.
Linear provides features such as issue tracking, reviews, cycles, initiatives, projects, and AI‑powered agents within a single interface.
Linear Docs provides an overview of Linear's features, integrations, and usage instructions.
Linear allows users to import issues from existing issue trackers into Linear.
Linear supports GitHub automations that link issues and automate pull request workflows.
Linear enables creation of custom issue workflows with new statuses.
Linear provides bulk actions to select and act on multiple issues at once.
Linear allows defining issue relations such as blocked, blocking, related, and duplicate.
Linear offers display options to order, sort, group issues and projects, and customize views.
Linear includes a triage inbox for issues from integrations and other teams.
Linear supports parent and sub-issues to break down larger tasks.
Linear provides notification settings to manage updates and stay informed.
Linear allows creation of teams within a workspace to organize different work functions.
Linear announced a new Team documents feature on June 4, 2026.
Every team now has a dedicated page to keep its context organized and easy to access.
Teams can create documents directly from their home page, add existing documents from projects and issues, and group resources into sections.
Linear Agent now supports sharing skills with a team, allowing reusable instructions to become shared team processes.
Issue description comments now allow users to comment directly on selected text or images within the description.
Linear's Free plan costs $0 per month.
Linear's Basic plan costs $10 per user per month, billed yearly.
Linear's Business plan costs $16 per user per month, billed yearly.
Linear's Enterprise plan has custom pricing with annual billing only.
The Free plan includes unlimited members, 2 teams, and 250 issues.
The Basic plan includes unlimited members, 5 teams, and unlimited issues.
The Business plan includes unlimited members, unlimited teams, and unlimited issues.
The Enterprise plan includes unlimited members, unlimited teams, and unlimited issues.
The Free plan allows file uploads up to 10 MB.
The Basic, Business, and Enterprise plans allow unlimited file uploads.
The Enterprise plan includes SAML and SCIM support, granular admin controls, invoice/PO billing, priority support, and account management.
Linear is trusted by more than 33,000 companies.
Linear powers more than 33,000 organizations, ranging from startups to Fortune 20 companies.
Customers report a 2.0x increase in filed issues after adopting Linear.
Customers report a 3.3x faster issue resolution after adopting Linear.
28% of issues are authored by agents in Linear deployments.
OpenAI chose Linear and scaled it to 3,000 users.
Coinbase uses Linear for agent‑first development.
Ramp’s coding agent contributed to 60% of merged pull requests using Linear.
Automattic migrated to Linear to unify its product teams.
Brex validated Linear’s impact through a data‑driven pilot.
Scale reduced bug resolution time by 52% after adopting Linear.
Remote switched its 1,000‑person team to Linear to move faster.

Vercel, Pleo, Mercury, Cash App, Clay, Perplexity AI, Substack, Clio, Monzo, Descript, Netlify, Retool, Supercell, Cohere, Raycast, Semgrep, Hex, OpenSea, Boom, Lightricks, Alan, and many others are listed as Linear customers.
OpenAI was founded in 2015 in San Francisco.
OpenAI has a company size of 5,300 employees.
OpenAI employs over 3,000 people.
Linear was initially adopted by a small trial of about 100 seats at OpenAI.
Linear usage at OpenAI grew to over 2,000 people across the organization.
Linear is used by more than 3,000 users at OpenAI overall.
OpenAI engineers work on up to eight different products in a single year.

verdict.bull: Strong adoption by leading tech companies, quantifiable productivity gains (3.3x faster resolution), and innovative agent features position Linear well in the market.
verdict.bear: The market is highly competitive with entrenched incumbents, and reliance on AI agent workflows may pose a long-term risk if the trend shifts.

risk_matrix.reason: Strong customer logos and feature set, but the project management market is crowded with established competitors like Jira and Asana.
Advanced engineering features are agent-centric, which may limit appeal to teams not yet adopting AI-driven development.
Impressive growth and productivity metrics, but the high proportion of agent-authored issues (28%) could indicate dependency on a trend that may not be sustained.

section_summaries.overview: Linear is a project management platform with tiered pricing (Free to Enterprise) and features like custom workflows, integrations, and team documents, used by high-profile tech companies such as OpenAI, Coinbase, and Ramp.
section_summaries.engineering: Linear offers engineering-focused capabilities including GitHub automations, shared agent skills, and inline commenting on issue descriptions.
section_summaries.traction: Linear serves over 33,000 organizations, with reported outcomes of 2x more issues filed, 3.3x faster resolution, and 28% of issues authored by agents.
